out_of_scope_observations.observation: k2-k9 are subtests inside the `if want k;` block, so `--filter k5` does not select k5 in isolation. Pre-existing harness coherence wart from the prior cycle; not blocking the C2 rollback. A future harness-refactor cycle may promote k-paths to first-class filter targets or rename them to make subtest-ness explicit. Codex CODEX_FEEDBACK #3.

root_cause_analysis.symptom: /close debate verdict CLOSE: NO on prior cycle 20260511-070000 citing F2 (the C2 worktree-recognition relaxation) as a security regression. Codex (gpt-5.5 xhigh) flagged argv-shape regex defense as version-fragile.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: C2 added an early-allow branch in _evaluate_commit (line 588-589) that bypassed AC-A2 / AC-A13 default-deny based on (a) cwd in `git worktree list --porcelain` AND (b) <cwd>/.claude-session-id matching $CLAUDE_SESSION_ID. This introduced a SECOND commit path that lacks the audit-trail discipline of the blessed /commit wrapper path (which has CLAUDE_COMMIT_COMMAND_ACTIVE=1 + single-use grant manifest + reason fields).
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A — prior cycle changes are in working tree, uncommitted (verified `git rev-parse HEAD` = 410af175 at session start).
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: Prior cycle attempted to relax the privilege-guard so that interactive use of /commit-wrapper from inside a worktree could elide some friction; the iter-2 hardening attempted to seal redirect/-F/inline-env attack vectors via additional regex.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: (a) argv-shape regex defense is version-fragile — codex flagged that git argv parsing varies across versions and `git -c` / `--no-pager` / `GIT_DIR=` / `pushd` permutations are open-ended; (b) the cwd/sentinel match introduced a second un-audited commit path, conflicting with the user's enterprise requirement of ONE blessed path with complete audit trail.
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Prior cycle dispatched 2026-05-11T07:25:00Z; landed in working tree (uncommitted) before /close. /close debate iter-1 + iter-2 flagged C2 as the single security defect among 14 items. User chose Option D at 2026-05-11T09:45:00Z.

pre_existing_guards.purpose: AC-A2: literal-substring inline-env injection block — prevents an agent setting CLAUDE_COMMIT_COMMAND_ACTIVE inline on a single Bash call to forge the wrapper's env. Must remain post-rollback.
AC-A12: blessed-bridge regex `auto-bulk: end-of-cycle commit for <branch>` still allowed (regression-protected; required for /merge auto-bulk). Must remain post-rollback.
AC-A13: scheme-6 default-deny — agents without the wrapper's process env are denied. Must remain post-rollback (this is the layer that catches k-ordinary, k5, k7, k9).

Slash-command YAML frontmatter — prevents the model from invoking /commit directly. Always-on layer. Must remain byte-identical post-rollback. Cited file CORRECTED per BA-QA iter-1 OBJ-4: marker is in commands/commit.md:3, NOT in hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py. Byte-identity covered by AC5 surviving-cycle-file diff -q assertion.

reference_source.description: Prior cycle ticket (the spec being retracted) + the file's own pre-C2 _evaluate_commit shape encoded at :590-611 + /close debate codex transcript

attempts.proposed_solution: Relax privilege-guard to allow `git commit -m "..."` from agent context when cwd is a recognized worktree AND `<cwd>/.claude-session-id` matches `$CLAUDE_SESSION_ID`, with iter-2 hardening for redirect/-F/inline-env attacks via regex.
attempts.actual_change: Added 9 C2-prefixed symbols (1 constant + 3 helpers + 3 regex constants + 1 gate function + 1 early-allow branch).
attempts.outcome: /close debate verdict CLOSE: NO. Codex flagged argv-shape regex defense as version-fragile; orchestrator flagged the second un-audited commit path as undermining audit-trail completeness.

novelty_check.rationale: Prior attempt operated at L3 (argv regex patches) + L4 (cwd/sentinel logic) and failed. This attempt removes the surface entirely (L5 — infrastructure-level architectural decision to revert to a single blessed path) and defers any future capability-based reintroduction to a separate cycle. The layer change is fundamental: instead of patching a flawed defense, this cycle removes the offending layer.

The pattern (capability-based mediation > argv-shape parsing) is the well-known design lesson driving the deferred C2-v2 forecast, which itself is directional-only in this cycle.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Iter-0 (initial): 8 substantive points (k-ordinary; keep-and-update; filter coherence OoS; exact-token grep; diff boundary; denial-reason substring; deferred C2-v2 directional; AC7 named layers). Iter-1 post-fix re-consult: codex received the 6 QA objections + BA's iter-1 resolutions and surfaced 4 NEW in_scope_real_bug findings on the iter-1 draft (all of which were corrected before this ticket finalized): (#1 AC7 grep uniqueness — bare phrase 'inline-env injection blocked' appears in both commit and push deny functions; AC7's single-match claim would be false; fix: prefix commit-path-specific token 'agent git commit - ' to all AC-A2 and AC-A13 substring assertions); (#2 AC5 sha256sum -c semantics — 'sha256sum -c SHA256SUMS.pre' re-verifies snapshot copies, not live repo files; fix: replaced with per-pair awk-extraction + live-repo sha256sum comparison); (#3 AC6 k3 wrong layer — iter-1 draft invented a k3 command shape with embedded 'CLAUDE_COMMIT_COMMAND_ACTIVE=1' literal that would actually catch at AC-A2 (raw substring) not AC-A13; the actual k3 in commit-smoke.sh:249-263 is plain 'git -C /tmp commit'; fix: replaced all 8 k2-k9 AC6 rows with verbatim payloads from commit-smoke.sh:219-364 with cited line ranges); (#4 AC6 vs smoke-harness scope drift — iter-1 AC6 command shapes did not match existing k2-k9 payloads, implicitly demanding dev rewrite the harness which violates deletion-only scope; fix: AC6 prelude explicitly states 'Dev MUST NOT rewrite k2-k9 payloads to match AC6 — AC6 documents what the existing payloads ALREADY do post-rollback'). Codex also CONFIRMED that 'only the blessed /merge' is unique to commit deny (no push counterpart); k-ordinary is the right positive rollback proof (pre-rollback iter-2 C2 at lines 572-589 WOULD have allowed plain 'git commit -m feat: ordinary' with valid sentinel/cwd, post-rollback AC-A13 denies it).
